objectives: Identify and use direct object pronouns (me, te, lo, la, nos, los, las)
Identify and use indirect object pronouns (me, te, le, nos, les)
Place object pronouns correctly before conjugated verbs
Avoid redundant noun repetition using pronouns

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Nadia
dialogue_blocks.text: ¿Le compraste el regalo a tu mamá?
dialogue_blocks.translation: Did you buy your mom the gift?
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [le kompɾˈaste el reˈɣalo a tu maˈma]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Tomás
dialogue_blocks.text: Sí, se lo compré ayer. Es una bolsa muy bonita.
dialogue_blocks.translation: Yes, I bought it for her yesterday. It's a very nice purse.
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [si ‖ se lo komˈpɾe aˈʝeɾ ‖ es ˈuna ˈβolsa muj βoˈnita]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Tomás
dialogue_blocks.text: Todavía no. La voy a envolver esta noche.
dialogue_blocks.translation: Not yet. I'm going to wrap it tonight.
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [toˈðaβia ˈno ‖ la ˈβoj a embolˈβeɾ ˈesta ˈnoʧe]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Nadia
dialogue_blocks.text: ¿Y a tu papá? ¿Le vas a comprar algo?
dialogue_blocks.translation: And your dad? Are you going to buy him something?
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [i a tu paˈpa ‖ le βas a kompɾˈaɾ ˈalɣo]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Tomás
dialogue_blocks.text: Sí. Le quiero comprar una corbata. Lo vi en la tienda la semana pasada.
dialogue_blocks.translation: Yes. I want to buy him a tie. I saw it at the store last week.
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [si ‖ le ˈkjeɾo kompˈɾaɾ ˈuna koɾˈbata ‖ lo ˈβi en la ˈtjenða]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Nadia
dialogue_blocks.text: Mira, yo te ayudo a escogerla si quieres.
dialogue_blocks.translation: Look, I'll help you choose it if you want.
dialogue_blocks.ipa: [ˈmiɾa ‖ jo te aˈʝuðo a eskoˈxeɾla si ˈkjeɾes]

dialogue_blocks.speaker: Tomás
dialogue_blocks.translation: Thanks! I appreciate it a lot.

srs_flashcards.back: I give it to him/her (le+lo → se+lo)

exercises.instruction: Escoge la oración correcta (Choose the correct sentence)
exercises.question: Your friend asks 'Did you see the movie?' How do you say 'Yes, I saw it'?

feedback.correct: ¡Correcto! 'La vi' — 'la' replaces 'la película' (feminine direct object) and comes BEFORE the verb.
feedback.incorrect: 'La vi' is correct. The direct object pronoun 'la' (for feminine nouns) goes before the conjugated verb. 'Le' is indirect object. Pronouns don't follow the verb in this position.

exercises.instruction: Une el pronombre con su función (Match the pronoun to its function)
Reemplaza el objeto con el pronombre correcto (Replace the object with the correct pronoun)

dialogue.speaker: Rosa
dialogue.text: ¿Llamaste a tu hermano? — Sí, _____ llamé ayer. (him)

dialogue.speaker: Rosa
dialogue.text: ¿Le mandaste el mensaje a Ana? — Sí, _____ _____ mandé. (to her / it-m)

dialogue.speaker: Felipe
dialogue.text: ¿Nos compraste los boletos? — Sí, _____ compré para ustedes. (them-m)

speaking_rubric.title: ¿Lo hiciste? (Did you do it?)
speaking_rubric.scenario: Your friend asks whether you did various tasks — called someone, bought something, sent a message. Answer each using the correct object pronoun.
speaking_rubric.prompt: Responde a las preguntas usando pronombres de objeto directo e indirecto.

sample_response.text: Sí, lo llamé esta mañana. Le mandé un mensaje también. Las llaves las tengo aquí, no te preocupes.
sample_response.ipa: [si ‖ lo ʝaˈme ˈesta maˈɲana ‖ le manˈðe um menˈsaxe tamˈbjen]

cultural_notes.formality: In casual Mexican Spanish, pronoun doubling is common: '¿A Juan lo viste?' (Did you see Juan?) — the object Juan is mentioned AND replaced by 'lo'. This is natural, not redundant.
cultural_notes.gestures: Pointing while using pronouns is natural in conversation — pointing to an object while saying 'lo' or 'la' helps clarify reference.
cultural_notes.regional_variations: In Mexico (and most of Latin America), 'lo' is used for masculine people and things. Spain uses 'le' for masculine people (leísmo). Stick with 'lo' for people and things in Mexican/Latin American Spanish.
